items.text:
Subject: Welcome to the Riverside Campus Makerspace

Dear New Member,

Welcome to the Riverside Campus Makerspace, located on the ground floor of Keller Hall in Room 110. We're thrilled to have you join our growing community of student builders, artists, and inventors. The space is open Monday through Friday from 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM, and on Saturdays from noon until 4:00 PM.

Before operating any of the laser cutters or 3D printers, you must finish a brief safety training session. These sessions run every Tuesday at 5:00 PM, and no appointment is needed. A one-time materials deposit of $20.00 is collected at your first visit; it is fully refunded once you return your equipment key at the end of the semester.

Please remember to sign in at the front desk and to clear your station before leaving. If anything is unclear, stop by during open hours or email the front office.

We can't wait to see what you create.

Best regards,
Dana Whitfield
Makerspace Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Welcome to the Riverside Campus Makerspace

questions.stem: According to the email, what must new members do before operating the laser cutters?

options.A: To pay a refundable twenty-dollar materials deposit
options.B: To finish a brief safety training session
options.C: To reserve a workstation at the front desk
options.D: To return an equipment key to the office

questions.explanation: The email states that before operating any of the laser cutters or 3D printers, members must finish a brief safety training session held every Tuesday. The deposit, signing in, and returning the key are mentioned elsewhere but are not the requirement tied to using the equipment.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the materials deposit?

options.A: It is charged again at the start of every semester
options.B: It covers the cost of all printing supplies
options.C: It is returned once the borrowed key is handed back
options.D: It must be paid in cash at the front desk

questions.explanation: The email says the one-time deposit is fully refunded once the equipment key is returned at the end of the semester, so it can be inferred that getting the deposit back depends on handing the key back. The other options reverse this rule or add details the email never mentions.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To announce the makerspace's new weekend hours
options.B: To remind members to tidy their work stations
options.C: To advertise a paid laser-cutting course
options.D: To welcome new members and explain getting started

questions.explanation: The email greets new members and then walks them through hours, the required safety session, and the deposit, so its overall purpose is to welcome them and explain how to begin. The other choices each capture only a single detail or describe a purpose the email does not serve.
